files.description: Binding repo contract. Declares the THIN SHELL rule (no decision logic here — it lives in the published @intentsolutions/rollout-gate package), the five non-negotiable CISO bindings carried from ISEDC DR-004 (predicate URI immutability, DNSSEC/CAA Rekor pre-condition, labs.intentsolutions.io reserved-don't-touch, credential redaction, synthetic fixtures / no partner names), and the fail-closed contract. Treat its rules as authoritative when reviewing any change.

Public face of the Action. States v0.3.0 status, the four-step behavior (read bundle → resolve policy → delegate decide() to @intentsolutions/rollout-gate@2.0.0 → report), the fail-closed-everywhere contract, the full inputs/outputs tables, the quickstart uses: examples, and the thin-shell rule. Version strings here (status banner, uses: pins, capability notes) must reconcile with action.yml, version.txt, and package.json.

The GitHub Action manifest — the wire contract. Declares inputs (bundle-path, policy-path/policy-json, fail-on-block, plus reserved v0.0.x inputs predicate-uri/rekor-url/cosign-key and deprecated aliases policy-file/dry-run), outputs (decision/reasons/summary/signed-decision-row-path), runs.using node24 → dist/index.js, and the consumed predicate URI default https://evals.intentsolutions.io/gate-result/v1. Reserved-input descriptions and version notes must stay reconciled with version.txt, package.json, and README.md.

Main architecture design record. The authoritative narrative: what the gate consumes (Evidence Bundle gate-result/v1 rows), the decision tier that sits ABOVE the bundle, the verify → evaluate → emit stages, the signed rollout-decision/v1 attestation, the OTel events fired, and § 5 CISO + compliance bindings (predicate URI immutability, evals-only attestation surface, labs reserved, credential redaction, synthetic fixtures). Source-of-truth design when reviewing any decision-flow or predicate-handling change.

NORMATIVE per-repo blueprint (binding_authority iar-E01) — the single source of truth for this repo's identity, architecture, boundaries, and Definition of Done. Inherits Blueprint A principles + anti-goals, declares which Blueprint B canonical entities it touches, pins peer-repo dependency ranges (kernel, decision package, audit-harness), and names the repo-specific non-goals (re-implementing decision logic; making novel code-quality attestations) whose violation triggers an ISEDC re-convene. Read for whether a change respects the repo's locked boundaries.

DR-002 — the runtime-language decision record. RATIFIED: TypeScript-primary, recording the upstream ISEDC DR-010 § 13.5 TS-primary-for-signing-surfaces lock. Supersedes the 'deferred to first M5 PR' callout in the 001 design doc. Its § 6 names the five v0.1.0-experimental → v0.2.0 acceptance criteria. Cite when a change touches runtime/build-target choices or the experimental→stable contract gates.

AT-SPEC acceptance / verification record (E08). VERIFIED that the three out-of-repo artifacts gating the v0.1.0-experimental → v0.2.0 transition are present and locked before v0.2.0: the Evidence Bundle SPEC normative requirements R14–R18 (the consumption interface this action implements), the kernel gate-result/v1 JSON Schema, and the DNSSEC/CAA Rekor pre-flight. Reference when reviewing changes to the consumption contract or schema-source.

Acting-head-of-board sign-off decision record that ratifies the 006 normative-lock verification — the governance approval authorizing the consumption-contract freeze ahead of v0.2.0. Read for who authorized the contract freeze and under what conditions.

Release report — migration notes for the v0.1.0-experimental → v0.2.0 transition. Documents that the public uses: interface is forward-compatible (inputs/outputs additive-only per Evidence Bundle SPEC R18) with no breaking change across the transition. Reference when a PR alters the input/output surface — additive-only is the rule.

Canonical testing-policy spec. Classifies the repo as a GitHub Action thin delegate shell whose suite is FIXTURE-DOMINANT (assert the exact emitted decision for a bundle+policy; do NOT re-test the upstream decision algebra or the kernel predicate schema). Declares the coverage/branch/mutation thresholds escape-scan pins, the L1–L7 layer enforcement table, the mandatory test properties (fail-closed coverage, credential redaction, kernel-source-of-truth predicate URI, dist sync), and the CI gates (check / lint-action-yaml / smoke-action). Reference when reviewing test changes or threshold edits (a threshold edit requires a paired audit-harness init in the same commit).

Single-line current release marker (0.3.0). One of the four version surfaces (with action.yml, package.json, README.md) that must cite the same release; a bump here without the others is a drift to flag.

Security policy. Binds the security-relevant constraints that apply to this repo's eventual signed-attestation surface: predicate URI immutability and the DNSSEC pre-condition for any Rekor push. Reference when reviewing signing / attestation / supply-chain changes.
